name: Char-Broiled Turkey
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat charcoal grill to medium heat with most coals on the opposite end from where the turkey will be sitting.
2. Wash and rinse the turkey, removing giblets. Dry with absorbent towels and season inside with salt and pepper.
3. Rub oil on the outside of the bird.
4. Stuff the cavity with onions, celery, orange, apple, 2 limes, and 1 lemon quartered. Fill cavities to keep the turkey moist.
5. Melt butter in a roasting pan, add water, and sliced lemon (add more lemon juice if desired).
6. Cover the turkey tightly with aluminum foil, crimping edges, and cook over grill for 4-6 hours.
7. Remove foil and add smoker chips for the last hour or so.
8. Alternatively, stuff the bird, cover it with aluminum foil, and bake in a 350-degree oven for 6-7 hours.
